Name: Bee
Quote: Information is honey. I keep the hive buzzing.
Bio: Bee is a Nosferatu courier, relaying messages and secrets between the clan’s various warrens. She is quick and discreet, rarely drawing attention to herself. Bee’s reliability has made her indispensable to the Nosferatu network.

Name: I.Q.
Quote: Brains over beauty—every time.
Bio: I.Q. is a Nosferatu hacker, maintaining the clan’s digital networks and safeguarding their secrets. He is reclusive and eccentric, preferring the company of computers to people. I.Q.’s technical expertise is unmatched, making him a valuable asset to the Camarilla.

Name: Tommy
Quote: People don’t see me, but I see them. That’s how I stay alive.
Bio: Tommy is a Nosferatu scout, adept at gathering intelligence and avoiding detection. He is quiet and observant, preferring to let others do the talking. Tommy’s reports are prized by the clan’s leaders, who rely on his vigilance to keep the warren safe.

Name: Carol
Quote: Ugly is just another word for invisible. I see everything you wish I didn’t.
Bio: Carol is a Nosferatu information broker, her disfigurement granting her access to the city’s hidden places. She is a master of secrets, trading information for favors and protection. Carol’s network of spies is the envy of her clan, and even the Prince knows to tread carefully around her.

Name: Ian Rammond
Quote: The world above is a stage. Down here, we write the script.
Bio: Ian Rammond is a Nosferatu warren keeper, overseeing the clan’s subterranean domain. He is an expert in urban exploration, mapping the city’s forgotten tunnels and passages. Ian’s knowledge is invaluable to those who need to move unseen or hide from the light.

Name: Terry
Quote: Down here, everyone’s got a secret. I just know more than most.
Bio: Terry is a Nosferatu archivist, maintaining the clan’s extensive records of Kindred activity. He is meticulous and detail-oriented, rarely missing anything that happens in his domain. Terry’s archives are a valuable resource for those seeking information or leverage.

Name: Mylene 'the Puck' Hamelin
Title: Primogen
Domain:
Bennington Heights (Full)
Dupont by the Castle (Major)
Quote: Why?  Because if you don't I'll smash your fucking teeth in, that's why.
Bio: Mylene is the Ventrue primogen everyone grudgingly accepts, because she gets results and doesn’t play courtly games. A former solicitor with a foul mouth and zero patience, she enforces clan discipline personally when someone embarrasses the Blue Bloods. They call her “the Puck” because she hits like one: direct, brutal, and impossible to ignore. Popular, effective, and terrifyingly sincere, she’s the kind of rival you hate to face.

Name: Zuri Oluwusi
Domain:
Bennington Heights (Full)
Dupont by the Castle (Major)
Quote: Leadership is not a title. It’s a responsibility I bear gladly.
Bio: Zuri Oluwusi is a Ventrue diplomat, skilled in negotiation and conflict resolution. She is respected for her integrity and fairness, often serving as a mediator in Camarilla disputes. Zuri’s calm demeanor belies a steely resolve and a keen strategic mind.

Name: Adrian Varga
Domain:
Bennington Heights (Full)
Dupont by the Castle (Major)

Name: Sterling Siskin
Domain:
Bennington Heights (Full)
Dupont by the Castle (Major)
Quote: Every empire is built on discipline. Mine is no different.
Bio: Sterling Siskin is a Ventrue financier, managing the clan’s investments and ensuring a steady flow of resources. He is disciplined and detail-oriented, rarely making mistakes. Sterling’s influence extends throughout the Camarilla’s economic networks.

Name: Jessie Sharp
Domain:
Bennington Heights (Full)
Dupont by the Castle (Major)
Quote: Clanless doesn’t mean powerless. I make my own legacy.
Bio: Abandoned by her sire and left to navigate the treacherous world of Kindred politics alone, Jessie Sharp has learned to rely on her wits and adaptability. As a Caitiff, she is often underestimated, but Jessie has turned her lack of clan into a strength, mastering a variety of disciplines and forging alliances wherever she can. She is fiercely independent and harbors a quiet resentment toward the rigid clan hierarchies of the Camarilla.

Name: Anita Morris
Quote: My problems started when I got everything I wanted.
Bio: "... I mean look, we're monsters, we do shitty things.  Yeah, I think I'm better than most, so I figured I had karma to spare when an opportunity presented itself to... um... to return to the fold.  Besides, I saw the writing on the wall: No way can this madwoman and her thugs take on the Regent.  Especially while we're all looking over our shoulders because our food has fucking militarized..."

Name: Ulysses Celaj
Quote: Justice is a blade; sometimes it must be sharp, sometimes unseen. I am both.
Bio: Ulysses Celaj is a recent addition to the Camarilla’s Banu Haqim ranks, a neonate eager to prove his worth in a city that mistrusts his clan’s reputation. Raised in a tradition of silent judgment and swift retribution, Ulysses is methodical and patient, preferring to observe before acting. He is haunted by the weight of his clan’s past as assassins, striving to embody their new role as arbiters and enforcers of Kindred law.

Name: Gabriel Collard
Quote: Justice is not blind. She sees everything—and so do I.
Bio: Gabriel Collard is a Banu Haqim investigator, specializing in uncovering Kindred crimes and enforcing the Camarilla’s laws. He is tenacious and incorruptible, earning respect even from those who fear his clan’s reputation. Gabriel’s dedication to justice often puts him at odds with more pragmatic elders.

Name: The Aristocrat
Title: Harpy
Quote: Ta-daaa!
Bio: The Aristocrat is an unlikely Nosferatu in more ways than one: A consummate socialite with a loud and flamboyant personality, he flaunts the characteristic subtlety of his clan to stand tall in the spotlight.  This has made him one of Toronto's most popular Kindred, a welcome presence in Elysium and Anarch bars alike, with a circle of friends that transcends the boundaries of sect. Not only is he a respected Herald (or caustic Harpy, depending upon who you ask), he is widely thought of as the leader of Toronto's trio of Harpies.  Finding oneself on the receiving end of the Aristocrat's scorn is arguably the surest way to torpedo one's respectability in an avalanche of scorn.

Name: Xavier Whitchurch
Title: Harpy
Quote: It is not my *duty* to remind you of what you owe and to whom.  It is my *privilege* to make you into an example so persuasive that no one else will mistake the obligations of prestation for a dead custom of times gone by.
Bio: Of Toronto's three harpies, Xavier doesn't claim the spotlight with the same effervescent ease shared by the Aristocrat and Christianne.  But it is precisely because of Xavier's dedication to his work that his two coterie-mates are free to shine so brightly.  For it is a city's harpies who are the ultimate caretakers of prestation, the economy of favors owed and repaid, without which civil Kindred society may well collapse. Xavier's approach to upholding prestation is unconventional: Ill-tempered for detailed book-keeping, he instead elects for deterrence.  With the Puck's enthusiastic support, Xavier makes any violations of the custom very painful, very public, and utterly unforgettable.

Name: Christianne
Title: Harpy
Quote: No, you vacuous halfwit, he didn't *dump* me.  We wanted different things, is all: loyalty for me, treason for him.  Besides, I heard he's doing odd jobs for the Setites now?  I think they call that 'dodging a bullet'.
Bio: Christianne may be young among her Camarilla peers, but she is a Toronto native and has lived here her entire life.  Admired and feared for her rapier wit in equal part, she and the Aristocrat are the sort of harpy-partnership with the power to bring princes to their knees with a few choice words at Elysium.  Her cachet has only risen with the dramatic defection of the former sheriff Locke Ulrich to the Anarchs: they were assumed to be something of an item until then.  Though Christianne has hidden the truth with characteristic Toreador aplomb, it is no great secret that she was as blindsided as everyone else.

Name: Laz
Quote: Am I really the only one who gives a flying fuck about this city?
Bio: As Camarilla sheriff, Laz stands on the front lines of Toronto's defense: the responsibility for keeping the city secure ultimately rests on his shoulders.  But with so many Camarilla elders abandoning their domains for the Middle East, Laz is becoming desperate: desperate enough to pursue an alliance with the Anarchs, which has made him less than popular among the remaining Camarilla old guard.

Name: Osborne Lowell
Quote: Do you think I enjoy overseeing this shell of a former city?  I serve because I must.  I serve because no one else will.
Bio: Under the Iron Praxis, no one would have predicted that this unassuming ancilla would eventually rule the city as prince.  He was known and respected, of that there is no doubt, but his ambitions were less grand than most---especially among the Ventrue.  One may assume that his eventual (and, by all outward appearances, reluctant) rise to the throne was the result of a compromise among those older and more powerful: the choice that ruffled the fewest feathers, a candidate who could be kept on a short leash.  UPDATE: Osborne Lowell was forced to step down as then-Seneschal Frederik Scheer seized Praxis and began his brutal reign, which was ultimately ended by Locke Ulrich.  He currently resides in Bennington Heights, staying out of Camarilla politics.

Name: Emily, the Dusk Rose
Quote: Shh, pet. Such luscious hair, such beautiful skin, such wide, wide eyes. I think I'll keep you, as our little secret.
Bio: Subtle, deadly, and sadistic, the knowledge that it is Emily who serves Sheriff Laz as Scourge (and not Raphael Bishop) has spread only slowly through the Camarilla and Toronto's Kindred community at large.  Beautiful but for the tears of thick, foul-smelling ichor that continuously fall from her eyes, she is uniquely capable among the Nosferatu of being seen in public without threatening the Masquerade.  There is no doubt that her clan takes full advantage of this, positioning the Dusk Rose as the Nosferatu most-frequently called upon whenever it is necessary to treat with the kine directly.

Name: Father Emiliano Diaz
Quote: Confess your sins, or I will drag them into the light for you.
Bio: Father Emiliano Diaz is a Lasombra confessor, using his powers to extract secrets and enforce discipline within the Camarilla. He is stern and uncompromising, feared for his ability to expose hidden truths. Emiliano’s faith is genuine, but his methods are often harsh.

Name: Deacon Lucenzo Reál
Quote: Fear of the dark is a sensible instinct.  If I were you, I'd be terrified.
Bio: Deacon Kevin Choice is a recent Lasombra defector to the Camarilla, struggling to earn the trust of his new allies. He is both a priest and a predator, wielding his clan’s mastery over darkness to enforce order. Kevin’s sermons are as inspiring as they are intimidating, and he is determined to prove that the Lasombra can be more than just monsters of the night.

Name: Sister Genoveva Cicione
Quote: The night is my cathedral. I worship in silence and shadow.
Bio: Sister Genoveva is a Lasombra mystic, blending her faith with her clan’s mastery of darkness. She is contemplative and enigmatic, often retreating to meditate in solitude. Genoveva’s devotion to both God and the Camarilla sets her apart from her more ruthless kin.

Name: Victor Vex
Quote: It seemed like the right idea at the time: I'm not really a 'Council of Primogen' kind of guy.  Now, what can I get you?
Bio: Vex is considered tedious by most, in part because of his firm belief in the power of his Evil Eye—and his propensity to use it whenever he is challenged.  As the youngest member of Prince Steele's Council of Primogen, Vex managed to accomplish a great deal despite his delusions: He negotiated control over the Waterfront and established Clan Malkavian’s grip over most of Toronto’s tourism industry and ports of entry.  For reasons unknown, he abdicated from his position on the council when Bertrice stepped down; tonight, he attends Elysia with no greater fanfare than any other ancilla—but for his position as Toronto's primary liason with the Circulatory Network.

Name: Georgie St. James
Quote: ha HA ha HA HA ha ha
Bio: Georgine Tell is infamous for her dark sense of humor and her penchant for practical "jokes" that only she finds funny — and hysterically so. Unpredictable and chaotic, she nevertheless serves as Vex's right-hand woman in overseeing the Moon Club (as is often the case when Malkavians work together, things "just work"). Georgine’s curse manifests as compulsive, mocking laughter, often aimed at precisely the right pressure point to drive another Kindred to frenzy... or simply to blow her own Obfuscate.

Name: Vance Vogel
Quote: My hand on God's ear, you can believe me when I tell you ...
Bio: Vance Vogel is Vex's own childe, and is said to be ... "close" with his sire. The truth has proven hard to untangle, for Vance's curse is to lie, and lie well: he crafts elaborate delusions replete with layers of deception backed up with obsessive detail, and it's never clear whether Vance himself falls for them. To hear him tell it, he was either Embraced out of pure love, or because Vex appreciated his initials. Likewise, he is either a gifted prophet or a creative storyteller, a Machiavellian prodigy or an impulsive exaggerator. Vance is often at odds with the Camarilla’s expectations, but his unique perspective sometimes reveals solutions others overlook.

Name: Scott Tempo
Quote: Yo, yo, yo! We're all still friends here, yeah?
Bio: Far from being defined by his lineage, Vex has spun Scott's weak blood into an asset — one that is far more welcome among Anarchs and the thin-blooded than Kindred of purer blood could ever hope to be. Indeed, by design, Scott has earned himself and Vex's enterprise access to Anarch dive bars, duskborn cooks, outcast blood sorcerers, and other unconventional avenues for Vex to exploit. Ambitious to a fault, Scott’s determination to prove himself to his coterie has caused Vex headaches in the past, and skirted the Traditions more than once.

Name: Oliver Gagnon
Quote: Beauty is fleeting, but the memory of it is eternal. I collect both.
Bio: Oliver Gagnon is a renowned patron of the arts, his gallery a haven for mortal and Kindred artists alike. He is driven by a hunger for inspiration, surrounding himself with beauty and passion to stave off ennui. Oliver’s charm masks a calculating mind, always searching for the next muse—or the next pawn in Camarilla politics.

Name: Scarlett
Quote: Every performance is a confession. Listen closely, and you’ll hear my sins.
Bio: Scarlett is the childe of Oliver Gagnon, inheriting her sire’s love of the arts and his taste for intrigue. She is a celebrated performer in Kindred and mortal circles, using her beauty and talent to captivate audiences and mask her own ambitions. Scarlett is fiercely loyal to Oliver but yearns to step out of his shadow.

Name: Adam Whitton
Quote: Legacy is everything. I intend to leave mine in stone.
Bio: Adam Whitton is a Ventrue architect, responsible for designing many of the Camarilla’s most secure havens. He is meticulous and visionary, always thinking several steps ahead. Adam’s ambition is matched only by his pride in his work.

Name: Alden Fulham
Quote: Every spell has a price. Are you willing to pay it?
Bio: Alden Fulham is a Tremere ritualist, specializing in the study of blood magic’s hidden costs. He is cautious and meticulous, always weighing the risks before casting a spell. Alden’s expertise is sought by those desperate for power—or desperate to avoid its consequences.

Name: Dr. Angelique Mooreland
Quote: Science and sorcery are two sides of the same coin. I flip it nightly.
Bio: Dr. Mooreland is a Tremere researcher, dedicated to bridging the gap between modern science and ancient magic. She is innovative and ambitious, often pushing the boundaries of what is considered possible—or safe. Angelique’s experiments have earned her both praise and suspicion within the clan.

Name: Vanda Overley
Quote: Knowledge is the only true currency. Everything else is borrowed time.
Bio: Vanda Overley is a scholar and ritualist, fiercely devoted to restoring the Tremere’s lost unity after the Pyramid’s fall. She is secretive and methodical, rarely seen without a tome or scroll in hand. Vanda’s loyalty to the clan is unwavering, and she is determined to rebuild its reputation within the Camarilla.

Name: The Island Devil
Title: Autarkis
Quote: Exile is freedom, if you know how to use it.
Bio: Known only as the Island Devil, this Nosferatu has made a home on the city’s abandoned outskirts. He is a master of survival, thriving in isolation and using his knowledge of the land to evade both Kindred and mortal enemies. The Island Devil is a boogeyman to some, a guardian to others.

Name: Bertrice
Title: Autarkis
Quote: To hunt, to soar, to fear neither man nor beast. This is what it means to be a vampire.
Bio: Beloved by the harpies, a rapier wit in Elysia, and possessed of beauty so refined she stuck an ever-present thorn in Primogen Bellanger’s side, Bertrice is as far from the typical Gangrel as one can get: her affinity for boardrooms and salons ensured that she continued to be welcome on the Council of Primogen long after Justicar Xavier announced the exodus of Clan Gangrel from the Ivory Tower. Recently incensed by Prince Lowell's favourable treatment of Clan Brujah, she abandoned her title and departed for the north of the city. Whether she intends to stay loyal to the Camarilla or declare herself the city's third autarkis remains to be seen.

Name: Kit Edwards
Quote: Being thin-blooded means I have to work twice as hard for half the respect. Bring it on.
Bio: Kit Edwards is a resourceful Thin-Blood, using his unique abilities to support the Men-In-Black coterie. He is inventive and determined, always looking for ways to prove his worth to the Anarchs. Kit’s resilience and adaptability make him a valuable ally in times of crisis.

Name: Blythe Threeton
Quote: Revolution isn’t a moment—it’s a habit. I practice every night.
Bio: Blythe Threeton is a firebrand among the Camarilla’s Brujah, never content to accept the status quo. She is passionate about Kindred rights and often clashes with elders who dismiss her as a troublemaker. Blythe channels her clan’s legendary rage into activism, striving to change the system from within.

Name: Big Galen Mazurik
Quote: Give me a cause, and I’ll give you a war.
Bio: Big Galen is a Brujah enforcer, more comfortable with his fists than with words. He is fiercely protective of his allies and quick to anger when provoked. Galen’s loyalty is hard-won, but once earned, he will fight to the death for his friends.

Name: Brandon Fromm
Quote: Truth is a shattered mirror. I see every piece, and none of them fit.
Bio: Brandon Fromm is a Malkavian seer whose visions are as unsettling as they are accurate. He speaks in riddles and metaphors, often leaving others frustrated or unnerved. Despite his madness, Brandon’s insights have proven invaluable to the Camarilla, and some elders tolerate his eccentricities in exchange for his guidance.
Notes: Strongly suspected of diablerie, exiled instead of killed because Laz couldn't be sure, now seeks readmission

Name: Lexie Madi
Quote: Talk is cheap. Action is everything.
Bio: Lexie Madi is a Brujah agitator, known for her quick temper and quicker fists. She is passionate about justice and equality, often leading protests against perceived injustices within the Camarilla. Lexie’s defiance has earned her both admirers and enemies.

Name: Symeon Rustovich
Quote: This is my House. Within its walls, hospitality rewards courtesy, and courtesy, hospitality. It would be ever so unfortunate were this simple exchange to become ... complicated.

Name: Radu Milovan syn Rustovich
Quote: I will not choose fear or ignorance. I will not cower in terror of myself. I will not hate myself, nor reject myself. I am Radu Milovan syn Rustovich, and soon this world shall know me for what I truly am.

Name: Antoine Rustovich
Notes: Ancient fleshcrafted caretaker of the estate

Name: The Wretch
Notes: Pitiful creature locked in the cellar, on whom the younger Tzimisce practices his 'arts'

Name: Maxwell 'Max' Floyd
Quote: You must be pretty fucking desperate to come down here to treat with the trash.  That's fine by me.  I can work with desperate.
Bio: It's better to reign in Hell than to serve in Heaven, and Max has certainly carved out a hellish principality for himself and his small coterie of ill repute.  Until recently, the Jarvis Jacks controlled the largest Anarch domain in Toronto despite being the smallest Anarch coterie.  Sure, their territory is hardly sought after, but the Jarvis Jacks' lucrative grip on the city's crime is iron-clad --- and completely unopposed.

Name: Mr. Easy
Quote: I'm sorry, I broke another one.  But I already cleaned up!  And I promise to be more careful with the next one.
Bio: Mr. Easy is a sadist, a rapist, and a pedophile. This is no secret.  Why Max brought him into the Jarvis Jacks when no other coterie would have him is anyone's guess: he has the mind of a child and a soul as putrid as they come.  On top of that, he's taken to compulsively Embracing kine, despite the fact that his weak blood consistently fails to create true vampires.

Name: Twist
Quote: You think you know pain?  You think you know misery?  You don't.  But I do, and I'm a very very good teacher.
Bio: In life, Twist was a pampered playboy of one of Toronto's most successful crime families... until he ran afoul of Clan Nosferatu, and was cursed with the Embrace as punishment.  As his body twisted and bloated into the hideous monstrosity he is tonight, so to did his shallow, superficial soul.  Now, he exists to render the torture he feels every time he looks into a mirror onto others.  Misery, it would seem, does indeed love its company.

Name: Jane 'JD' Doe
Quote:
Last night I was Sister Selene, who proved unable to unburden her
petitioner of his 'delusions'. Tonight I will be Candy: perhaps the sins of
the flesh can do what God could not.
Bio: Jane Doe is not her real name, but that hardly matters:  She becomes someone new every night, adopting personas and aliases as easily as a new pair of shoes.  Even her closest allies aren't sure if they've ever met the "real" Brujah behind the ever-changing masks.  As boss of the Men-In-Black, Jane bears ultimate responsibility for upholding the Masquerade --- and the baroness has given her unprecedented latitude to do whatever she deems necessary to carry out this duty.

Name: Jason
Quote: Just a thought, but has anyone considered not murder?
Bio: In contrast to sharing his chosen name with a brutal horror movie monster, Jason lacks the feral nature typical of the Gangrel---indeed, he is among the more measured voices in the Anarch Movement.  This isn't to say the boy lacks teeth: His rivalry with Ben Blinker is legendary, fraught with frequent games of one-upsmanship that more than once have threatened to drive the Men-In-Black apart.

Name: Ben Blinker
Quote: Look into my pretty, pretty eyes.
Bio: Ben Blinker's contribution to the Men-In-Black is obvious: his command of Dominate, particularly impressive for a neonate, is invaluable to his coterie's principal responsibility of protecting the Masquerade.  That being said, Ben Blinker's penchant for causing as many messes as he cleans up is legendary; his need to "keep things interesting" is a problematic manifestation of his Curse of Malkav.

Name: Wallflower
Quote: Stop worrying.  We're going to make your problem go away, and we're going to have fun doing it!
Bio: Toronto's Anarchs have a penchant for ironic names, and Wallflower is no exception.  Combative, proud, and fiercely territorial, Wallflower is a dependable ally to have at your side: a blushing violet, she is not.  Her coterie-mates in the Men-In-Black play their mindgames of subtle manipulation; Wallflower prefers to make her problems bleed, by tooth and by claw.

Name: J
Quote: Stop talking.  It's not doing either one of us any good.
Bio: "J" hardly impresses at first glance (indeed, a running joke about his name is that it's the longest he can spell).  Of course, the fact that he has led such a large and violent group of Kindred so effectively for so long suggests there is much more to this Brujah than first impressions would suggest. It is, after all, in the interests of every vampire to manage expectations.

Name: Leah Hawk
Quote: Put your fangs away.  We're going to have a polite conversation, and then you're going to tell me everything I need to know.
Bio: Leah Hawk is as much a hunter as her Gangrel kin, though her quarry is unconventional:  The prey she stalks is information, leverage, and she runs it down with all the tenacity of a wolf.  As a member of the Nightwolves, Leah serves to balance her more blood-thirsty coterie-mates; without her (and Old Quentin), the Nightwolves would be hard-pressed to accomplish anything approaching subtlety.

Name: Reaper
Quote: Shhhhhh.  Sleep, now.  This doesn't have to hurt.
Bio: Reaper is as centered as he is vicious, approaching the grizly work at which he excels with Zen-like serenity.  And excel he does; the "Reaper" sobriquet was bestowed on him by J in recognition of the fact that he has ended the lives and unlives of more of the Anarch Movement's enemies than anyone else in the city --- and the body count continues to rise.

Name: Stalker Todd
Quote: Please, run.  Dawn approaches; you might even get away.
Bio: There is perhaps no more effective hunter among the Anarchs than Stalker Todd.  Patient, thorough, calculating and meticulous, once he has caught wind of his quarry, there is no escape.  He relishes the hunt, and its end even more --- each kill is a work of art; a statement that cannot be ignored; a warning to others who would stand against the baroness.

Name: Rosie
Title: Emissary
Quote: Your willingness to sit at my table tells me all I need to know.
Bio: A Nosferatu may seem an odd fit for the preeminent diplomat among the Anarchs in Toronto, but Rosie has at least fifty years of experience negotiating with dangerous predators --- and her record of success speaks for itself.  Emphasizing mutual self-interest and refreshing (if biting) authenticity, Rosie was always the obvious choice to lead Red Flag's overtures to the Camarilla on behalf of the Anarch Movement.

Name: Professor Ethan Keen
Quote:
In conclusion, I submit a seventy percent confidence in the
     legitimacy of the sheriff's professed good intentions.
Bio: Professor Ethan Keen is a peerless expert on the psychology of emotion, an impressive feat given he has been incapable of feeling emotions of any kind since his Embrace into the Clan of Malkav.  His rational analysis and piercing insights into the Kindred psyche are invaluable to Red Flag's diplomatic efforts, albeit on the back lines --- his calculating demeanor is an ill fit to the nuances of negotiation.

Name: Damien Abanda
Quote:
Appearances can be deceiving, yes, but they can also be impressive.
     Should we not aim to impress our supposed betters?
Bio: Damien Abanda has a chip on his shoulder: as the only Toreador Anarch in the city, he obviously considers himself to be the resident expert in etiquette, style, presentation and Camarilla sensibilities.  On this, he may even be right: Rosie herself defers to his opinions on such matters, even if she rarely allows his condescending attitude anywhere near the negotiations themselves.

Name: Calvin Wallace
Quote:
The Anarch Independence Movement is so much more than
     a neonate uprising! It is a revolution, and it will change the world!
Bio: A firebrand even by the standards of Clan Brujah, Calvin might seem to be an odd fit for a coterie like Red Flag, whose responsibilities require diplomacy and a light touch.  Nevertheless, he was among the first names Rosie requested of the baroness; it seems she felt his passion would serve Red Flag's agenda well.

Name: Ren
Quote:
Do not be afraid: the path to redemption always begins in the dark.
     Take my hand, and we will find the light together.
Bio: The enigmatic and rarely-seen sire and leader of the Redeemers, Ren sits at the center of Ministry activity in Toronto.  He effortlessly commands the unquestioning devotion of his childer, who speak his name with nigh Messianic fervor.  To hear them tell it, an hour of Ren's counsel is a boon worthy of a prince --- and there is evidence to support their assessment:  Throngs of kine living in the Danforth have been transformed by his teachings at Redemption House, and Ren has apparently tamed even the fierce independence of the strong-willed Anarch Kingston Black, the former boss of Masque.

Name: Alexandra
Quote:
I am here to help however I can.
     What can I do to put your mind at ease?
Bio: Of Ren's three childer, Alexandra is the beating heart that bridges the gulf between Kai's restless enthusiasm and Tyler's stoic pragmatism.  The quintessential exemplar of still waters that run deep, Alexandra is quiet and thoughtful; intensely focused and unsettlingly unreadable.  On one hand, she campaigned heavily to welcome Kingston Black into the close-knit Redeemer family; on the other, she appears to be the least interested in ingratiating herself with the Anarch population at large.

Name: Kai
Quote: Woo! What a night, am I right?  Same time tomorrow, then?
Bio: Of Ren's three childer, Kai is the relentlessly-free spirit that spurs ever-cautious Tyler to action and invests ever-tranquil Alexandra with conviction.  It was Kai who persuaded the Redeemers to fight on the night of Vykos' attack, in spite of the icy reception the Ministry had received from the Anarchs before then.  As the youngest of the Redeemers, Kai has yet to develop the innate paranoia characteristic of the Kindred; whether his eagerness to trust others is a strength or a weakness remains to be seen.

Name: Tyler
Quote:
Sex, drugs, rock n' roll. Don't give into one of them.
     Give in to 'em all... in moderation. That's the ticket.
Bio: Of Ren's three childer, Tyler is the rock that anchors Alexandra's lofty idealism and grounds Kai's more-reckless impulses.  The eldest of Ren's childer, Tyler is protective of his sire and wary of potential threats to his unlife; as such, he is more reluctant than his siblings to welcome others into their circle of trust.

Name: Kingston 'King' Black
Quote: This House holds secrets I need to know, and I am stronger than they think.
Bio: Kingston Black, known as 'King,' is a Brujah revolutionary, always seeking to uncover hidden truths and challenge authority. He is driven by a desire to protect his coterie, Redemption House, and to expose the hypocrisy of those in power. King’s determination and strength make him a formidable ally—and a dangerous adversary.

Name: Sage Sam
Bio: Cursed with a never-ending stream of prophecy that must be spoken aloud, Sage Sam wears a perpetual cloak of silence and communicates via sign language with the help of his mortal wife, Suyin, now almost ninety years of age.  As boss of the Line, the baroness has entrusted Sam with securing Toronto's borders and managing inter-city travel.

Name: Mason Schmidt
Quote: Union Station is half a million square feet of twisting corridors, dozens of entrances, and two hundred thousand daily travellers. Keeping unwanted visitors out of there? It's only part of our job.
Bio: There are downsides to having an incommunicative Malkavian prophet as your Boss, and inflexibility in the field is one of them. Mason Schmidt was Embraced to solve this problem. He had distinguished himself at the RMC military academy, where his keen strategic mind earned him top honors in military wargame exercises. Schmidt acts as the Line's tactical command, with full authority in the field to... "calibrate" Sage Sam's orders with the facts on the ground.

Name: Jack-be-Nimble
Quote: Head back now? There's still five minutes till sunrise!
Bio: If anyone can be said to be a true expert of the city, it's Jack-be-Nimble.  Jack was here a decade or two before the baroness rose to power, and he spent most of that time as a courier---a service that has become even more important in the wake of the Second Inquisition's increased scrutiny of the Internet.  Even the Piece-Taker's domain isn't off limits to Jack: He's the only Anarch who dares to visit the place.  Well, almost only.

Name: Yusef Shamsin
Bio: <childe of Zelios whose architectural talents are prized throughout the Anarchs: he designed the baroness' fortress-haven at Site: Lotus>

Name: Bacchus Giovanni
Title: Emissary
Quote: Death is a door, not a wall. I am the one who opens it for you.
Bio: Bacchus Giovanni is a necromancer of the Hecata, descended from a long line of death-dealers and spirit-callers. He is suave and urbane, but his dark sense of humor and unsettling presence make others uneasy. Bacchus traffics in secrets from both the living and the dead, and his services are sought by those desperate enough to pay the price.

Name: Alexander
Quote: Drink up.  What, you think I put something in it?
Bio: The young Sabbat Lasombra holds a rather significant distinction: He is the only known member of the Sword of Caine to establish himself in the city proper, away from Centre Island, thanks to the aid of a mysterious benefactor. What he intends to do with his unique position remains to be seen; it may be difficult for one so young to operate alone, but the Lasombra rarely go gently into the night.

Name: Sang-Froid
Quote: Fear is a tool. I wield it with precision.
Bio: Sang-Froid is a Sabbat Nosferatu, infamous for his psychological warfare tactics. He delights in spreading terror among his enemies, using his grotesque appearance and mastery of stealth to devastating effect. Sang-Froid’s loyalty to the Sabbat is absolute, and he is feared even among his own sect.

Name: Alistair Etrata
Title: Primogen (Former)
Quote: I am no Assamite.  Those you call Assamites are the enemies of my people.  Thralls to the blood god Ur-Shulgi, they cloister themselves at Alamut---for now.
Bio: Arriving in Toronto at the invitation of an Anarch is an unlikely start for a primogen. Nevertheless, he found himself in a city experiencing a power vacuum just as the Banu Haqim had been admitted into the Camarilla, and he exploited these circumstances adroitly---undoubtedly assisted by the strength of his old Blood. In a very short period of time, he demonstrated both his loyalty to the Camarilla and his ability to serve his clan as their primogen. His activities since have been subtle: either Alistair is wary of causing anyone to regret his appointment, or---more likely---he is guarding his intentions from everyone except, perhaps, his childe Sinclair.

Name: Agnes Bellanger
Title: Primogen (Former)
Quote: Darling, I've taken the liberty of cleaning up your mess.  I'll let you know when and how you can thank me.
Bio: Born a peasant girl, captured by pirates, and sold in Istanbul to her eventual sire, Agnes has lived a storied history that brought her to Toronto (then Fort York) in the 1800s.  After a century travelling Europe, she returned to the city in the 1970's and established herself in her former haven: the opulant palace of Casa Loma.  There, her pedigree, political prowess, and thirst for vengeance against the Sword of Caine saw her swiftly rise to power as the foremost vampire of the Clan of the Rose.  Agnes Bellanger continues to serve on the Council of Primogen, and oversees the city's most popular Elysium at the castle she has made her home.

Name: Drake
Title: Primogen (Former)
Quote:
You know all that you need to know.
     When this changes, you will know that, too.
Bio: The Oubliette.  The Whisper-Weaver.  The Vault.  Perpetually shrouded in sheer black silks, Drake speaks little, but when he does it is in the interests of everyone to pay attention:  He wields his secrets like a surgeon’s scalpel, leveraging them for maximum impact with prescient timing.  It is said that he claimed P.A.T.H, Toronto’s vast subterranean shopping complex, for Clan Nosferatu by uttering three words in confidence to Seneschal Scheer.  For these reasons and the myriad unconfirmed rumors that accompanied them, Drake is among the most feared Camarilla elders in the country: an inscrutable boogeyman whose ultimate motives are always in doubt.

Name: Frederik Scheer
Title: Seneschal (Former), Prince (Former)
Quote:
The Camarilla has held Toronto for four hundred years,
     and we shall hold it for four hundred more.
Bio: Frederik Scheer has been a driving force behind Camarilla control over Toronto for at least two hundred years. As seneschal to the indomitable Prince Steele, Scheer developed a reputation for ruthless loyalty to the Ivory Tower. Tonight, with a new and untested ancilla holding Praxis, his power has only grown --- causing many to wonder who really calls the shots in the city.

Name: Adrian Gerrard
Quote: I am not Theo Bell.  We grieve with our Ventrue brothers over the loss of Hardestadt, and will honor the legacy of Prince Steele for as long as you will have us.
Bio: As primogen, Gerrard served Prince Steele as a capable administrator, accountant, and power broker, claiming more influence over the investment and commercial banks of Toronto than any other Kindred.  Tonight, he remains the eldest Brujah in the city, and works diligently to restore his clan's former place in the Camarilla after the treachery of Theo Bell at the Conclave of Prague and his unceremonious expulsion from the Council of Primogen.

Name: Raphael Bishop
Title: Scourge (Former)
Quote: I don't enjoy all that I'm asked to do.  But loyalty to the Pyramid is a virtue---especially in the wake of its fall.
Bio: Raphael Bishop is a creature of his sire's making---both literally and figuratively.  Scheer kindled in his childe a zealous devotion to House Tremere and the Pyramid, and this fire has found plenty of fuel in modern nights: Bishop watched Vienna fall, taking the storied might of his clan along with it.  He remembers what Clan Tremere *was*, and he shares with his sire a deep shame for its loss... and the will to get it back.

Name: Monika Eulenberg
Title: Baroness (Former)
Quote: The Camarilla may outnumber us, but my eyes are everywhere.  And what do they see?  A city ripe for the taking.  It is only a matter of time until Toronto becomes the capital of a new Anarch Free State.
Bio: In life, Monika Eulenberg served the German Democratic Republic of Soviet-occupied East Berlin as an agent of the Ministry of State Security: the oppressive Stasi secret police.  In death, her natural caution and professional paranoia grew pathological under the Curse of Malkav, forging a politically-savvy operator who quickly earned the respect of her Anarch associates.  When the Beckoning created a power vacuum in Toronto, Monika seized the opportunity to declare herself baroness of the city and offered her aid to a reeling Camarilla --- while efficiently leveraging a select number of loyal coteries into a strong Anarch foothold in one of the crown jewels of North America. UPDATE: Monika Eulenberg was assassinated by Second Inquisition agents during the events of the preceding Toronto By Night Chronicle.

Name: Locke Ulrich
Title: Prince (Former)
Notes: former Prince, assassinated by one of the PCs (who it is is yet to be determined by me), which is what opened the power vacuum that allowed the PCs to rise to power.

Name: Old Quentin
Quote: Didn't your mother teach you it's rude to ask someone their age?
Bio: Old Quentin is perhaps the most mysterious Anarch in the city --- and with a baroness like Monika, that's saying something. Young in appearance and young at heart, he's charmingly cagey when it comes to questions about his age. Is he really, as some whisper, a mighty elder laying low? Or is he a mere neonate enjoying such grandiose suspicions? And his Malkavian lineage invites another question: Does he even know? Whatever the case, Old Quentin has served the baroness ably and loyally, and as long as he continues to do so, she's hardly one to point fingers at the peculiarities of others.
